title: PS-68982 The GroupId when updated for the user should update in PermissionProject table for the same user.
body: PS-68982 The GroupId when updated for the user should update in PermissionProject table for the same user. Before this change itt was fetching the non-updated GroupID from PermissionProject table and updating in the m_UserCache.
author: AbdulRahim1412

reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]
copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body: In local testing, I am not seeing the behavior that this is attempting to fix. I have asked for clarification and update of the bug. Once we get that, we can determine whether this is an appropriate fix or not.
reviews.author: kcostenbader

review_comments.body: This introduces a second call to `UpdateUserProjectPermission` when `GroupID` is present in `recordChange.Fields`. If `AnyProjectPermissionsChanged` is also true (and the default group is being edited), the method can run twice in the same transaction, adding extra DB work and potentially affecting counters like `user.ProjectPermissionsAdded`. It would be safer to compute `userGroupIdChanged` once (e.g. `recordChange.Fields?.ContainsKey(nameof(UserMaintenance.GroupID)) == true`) and call `UpdateUserProjectPermission` only once with an explicit flag, rather than in two separate blocks. Also, `TryGetValue(..., out var value)` is currently only used for its boolean result; use `out _` (or `ContainsKey`) to avoid the unused local.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `userGroupIdUpdated` is derived from `recordChange.Fields.ContainsKey(GroupID)` rather than whether the value actually changed. This can trigger a full PermissionProject rewrite (one stored-proc call per project permission) even for no-op updates. Consider capturing the previous `user.GroupID` before applying `recordChange` and only running the PermissionProject update when the group ID transitions to a different value.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This change introduces new behavior when a user's default `GroupID` changes without any explicit project-permission edits. There are existing integration tests for project permission GroupID updates, but none appear to assert that a default GroupID change correctly updates `PermissionProject` while preserving project-specific role overrides. Adding a targeted test for this scenario would help prevent regressions.
review_comments.author: Copilot

issue_comments.body: I shall merge once feature/improv branch is open for merging
issue_comments.author: AbdulRahim1412
